channel.title: Frontion News Headlines
channel.description: Breaking geopolitical news from Tier 1 sources

item.title: 🇺🇸 Trump-Backed Challenger Defeats Rep. Thomas Massie in Kentucky Primary
item.description: Rep. Thomas Massie lost his Republican primary to Trump-endorsed Ed Gallrein, the latest GOP lawmaker to fall after opposing the president. Massie had criticized the Iran war and pushed for release of the Epstein files.

item.title: 🇹🇭 Thailand Cuts Visa-Free Stay for Over 90 Countries
item.description: Thailand has announced it will revoke the 60-day visa-free exemption for citizens of 93 countries, citing security concerns. Nationals from the UK, Australia, US, China and others will now need to apply for visas for stays longer than 30 days.

item.title: 🚢 Filipino Crew Caught in Crossfire as Iran Attacks Ship in Hormuz
item.description: A Filipino crew of 23 risked the dangerous Strait of Hormuz transit after a month stranded, only to face Iranian gunfire. The Washington Post documents their ordeal amid the US-Iran conflict.

item.title: 🚨 US Sanctions Gaza Flotilla Organizers Amid Israeli Crackdown
item.description: The US Treasury Department imposed sanctions on four activists involved in Gaza-bound aid flotillas, accusing them of supporting Hamas. The sanctions come as Israel continues intercepting the latest fleet of vessels.

item.title: 🛢️ Aluminium Supply Crunch Threatens Asia Clean Energy Push as Iran War Hits Gulf Plants
item.description: Iranian missile strikes on key Gulf aluminium plants are disrupting production of the metal essential for solar panels and wind turbines, threatening Asia renewable energy goals.

item.title: 🇬🇱 Greenland Must Reach Deal with US on Trump Control Bid, PM Says
item.description: Greenlands prime minister stated the Arctic territory is obliged to find a solution with the United States regarding Trumps desire to control the strategic island.

item.title: 🏛️ Senate Advances Resolution to Limit Trump Iran War Powers
item.description: The US Senate advanced a war-powers resolution 50-47 with Republican defectors including Senator Bill Cassidy, a rare rebuke of the president amid growing unease over the 80-day war.

item.title: 🇱🇻 Russia Warns Latvia Over Ukrainian Drones, Threatens Retaliation
item.description: Russia UN Ambassador Vasily Nebenzya claimed Ukraine plans to launch drones from Baltic states, warning NATO membership would not protect them. The US rebuked the threats.

item.title: 🇨🇳🇷🇺 Putin Arrives in Beijing for Summit With Xi Following Trump Visit
item.description: Russian President Vladimir Putin landed in China for a two-day state visit to meet with Xi Jinping, just days after Trump's high-profile Beijing summit, as both leaders seek to project stability amid global tensions.

item.title: ⚔️🇨🇳 Chinese Military Secretly Trained Russian Forces for Ukraine War
item.description: China's armed forces covertly trained around 200 Russian military personnel late last year, with those soldiers now returning to fight in Ukraine, according to European intelligence agencies and documents seen by Reuters.

item.title: 🏛️🇺🇸 US Senate Advances Bill to End Iran War After Primary Defeat Flips Vote
item.description: The Senate advanced legislation to force Trump to withdraw from the Iran war, as Senator Cassidy flipped his vote following his primary loss, signaling growing congressional resistance to the conflict.

item.title: 💰📉 Federal Reserve to Hold Rates Steady as War-Driven Inflation Persists
item.description: Most economists polled by Reuters expect the Fed to avoid cutting interest rates this year, pushing rate reduction expectations into 2026 as the Iran conflict continues to pressure global energy prices and inflation.

item.title: 📊🇺🇸 Trump Approval Drops to 35% Amid Softening Republican Support
item.description: President Trump's approval rating fell to 35% in a Reuters/Ipsos poll, just above the 34% low of his presidency seen last month, as Republican support continues to soften.

item.title: 🇺🇸🇪🇺 Pentagon cuts US brigade presence in Europe, delays Poland deployment
item.description: The US is reducing its brigade presence in Europe and delaying a planned deployment to Poland, marking a significant shift in US military posture on the continent amid ongoing questions about American commitment to NATO allies.

item.title: 🇮🇷 Iran rejects US school strike claim as baseless fabrication
item.description: Tehran dismissed US accusations of targeting a school as a "baseless fabrication," saying targeting an educational institution during school hours would constitute a grave violation of international humanitarian law.

item.title: 🇺🇸🇨🇳💻 US House foreign affairs chair warns AI sales to China could make America a loser
item.description: House Foreign Affairs Chair Brian Mast warned that American firms must not let US AI technology reach the "wrong places," stressing the strategic importance of maintaining technological advantages over China.

item.title: 🇮🇷 Trump Cancels Planned Iran Attack After Gulf States Request Delay
item.description: President Trump postponed military strikes on Iran after Gulf nations requested time for negotiations. Iran submitted a revised 14-point proposal via Pakistani mediators, but key disputes over nuclear enrichment and the Strait of Hormuz remain unresolved.

item.title: 🇺🇦 Ukraine Shifts to Strategic Neutralization Strategy Against Russia
item.description: Ukraine is pursuing asymmetric warfare to degrade Russia economy and military infrastructure rather than costly territorial offensives. Deep strikes on Russian oil refineries and military facilities are disrupting Moscow war effort and challenging Russia spring offensive.

item.title: 🇮🇱🇸🇴 Somaliland and Israel Announce Mutual Embassies in Historic Move
item.description: Somaliland will open an embassy in Jerusalem while Israel establishes diplomatic presence in Hargeisa. The move follows Israel official recognition of the breakaway Somali regions independence.

item.title: 🇺🇸🇨🇳 US Charges 7 Chinese Executives in Global Container Cartel Crackdown
item.description: The US Justice Department charged executives from companies that manufacture 95% of the world's shipping containers for price-fixing. The move targets China's dominance over global logistics infrastructure.

item.title: 🇮🇷⚠️ Iran Warns of New Fronts as US Threatens Large-Scale Assault
item.description: Tehran threatened to open new conflict fronts after Washington warned of potential large-scale military operations. Trump said he was "an hour away" from striking Iran before Gulf allies requested delay.

item.title: 🇺🇦💔 UN: Over 15,800 Killed in Russia's War on Ukraine
item.description: The United Nations confirmed nearly 800 children are among the more than 15,800 people killed since Russia's full-scale invasion began in February 2022.

item.title: 🛢️💰 US Crude Inventories Fall 9.1 Million Barrels
item.description: API reported crude inventories fell by 9.1 million barrels, though stocks remain higher than last year. Oil prices stay above $100 as Iran conflict continues.

item.title: 🇪🇪 NATO Jet Shoots Down Ukrainian Drone Over Estonia
item.description: A Romanian F-16 deployed with NATO intercepted and downed a Ukrainian drone over southern Estonia. Ukraine apologized for the incident, while Russia threatened retaliation if Baltic countries allow their territory to be used for attacks.

item.title: 🇺🇸 US Plans to Shrink NATO Crisis Forces
item.description: The Trump administration will tell NATO allies it plans to reduce the pool of US military capabilities available for alliance defense during crises. The move reflects Trumps expectation that European nations take primary responsibility for continental security.
